snippet.title: The Secret Ingredients of Great Hospitality | Will Guidara | TED
snippet.description:
Restaurateur Will Guidara's life changed when he decided to serve a two-dollar hot dog in his fancy four-star restaurant, creating a personalized experience for some out-of-town customers craving authentic New York City street food. The move earned such a positive reaction that Guidara began pursuing this kind of "unreasonable hospitality" full-time, seeking out ways to create extraordinary experiences and give people more than they could ever possibly expect. In this funny and heartwarming talk, he shares three steps to crafting truly memorable moments centered in human connection – no matter what business you're in.

snippet.title: Does Alcohol Cause Cancer? -  Milton Mills, MD
snippet.description:
Does Alcohol Cause Cancer? -  Milton Mills, MD


Milton Mills, M.D

• https://www.preventionofdisease.org/milton-mills

Milton Mills, MD practices urgent care medicine in the Washington DC area, and has served previously as Associate Director of Preventive Medicine and as a member of the National Advisory Board, for Physicians Committee for Responsible Medicine (PCRM). He has been a major contributor to position papers presented by PCRM to the United States Department of Agriculture regarding Dietary Guidelines for Americans, and has been the lead plaintiff in PCRM’s class action lawsuit that asks for warning labels on milk.

Dr. Mills earned his medical degree at Stanford University School of Medicine, and completed an Internal Medicine residency at Georgetown University Hospital. He has published several research journal articles dealing with racial bias in federal nutrition policy. He frequently donates his time via practicing at free medical clinics, and travels widely, speaking at hospitals, churches and community centers throughout the country. He was featured in the recent attention-getting film “What the Health,” and will also appear in the upcoming film “The Silent Vegan.”

snippet.description:
You can view/play the full episode webpage here:  https://timetoshinetoday.com/podcast/ryanmathie/  


Ryan Mathie is a Scottish author, coach, and expert in personal and professional development who is best known for helping new and ambitious coaches build their businesses as the Co-Founder and CEO of ‘High-Performing Coach’, a company that he has led since 2019. He was born in the small town of Bellshill in North Lanarkshire, Scotland, and is the great-nephew of the legendary, Sir Matt Busby, CBE, KCSG who was a Scottish football player and manager of the world-famous English Football team, Manchester United.With more than 17,000 hours of training/development/experience, Ryan is a leading figure amongst the rise of new coaches in the industry and is regarded by his peers as one of the best, most highly trained, experienced coaches in the world. 

As well as his passion for helping people living conscious and limitless lives, Ryan loves dogs, playing golf, meditating, and being on time.
His first solo book, ’High-Performing Coach - How to Create High-Fee Clients & Build a Profitable Coaching Business from the Heart’, is due to be published in early 2022.

Quote:: The future of humanity is consciousness- Ryan Mathie 

Knowledge Nuggets and Take-Aways:

Coaching exists to help people see themselves clearly with more awareness, reflect and get them to see inwards
Learn to forgive yourself and others, heal your heart and open yourself to the possibility of falling in love
If you are fed up and done being a victim, done pretending, done not liking yourself and want to make a contribution, please let us make a warm introduction to Ryan!
Trust the process, you are exactly where you need to be, be patient 
Fun fact, Ryan means ‘Kingly’ in Irish

snippet.title: Invite Plants To Every Meal with Meal Prep Mason Jar Meals | Chef AJ LIVE! with Tiffany Wilkerson
snippet.description:
If you have ever had the opportunity to meet Tiffany, you will see why she is described as a “Plant-Based Gardener."  Her zeal to plant as many seeds of nutritional health and awareness is second to none. With some rooted knowledge/watered with life application, her desire is for everyone to blossom and grow into a better quality of life through food.

Her heart, experience, and education speak for themselves. She first began her transition in 2010 upon losing one of the most important people in her life to colon cancer, her grandmother, Dorothy. 

After her passing, it was then Tiffany immediately knew that her life must change, nutritionally.   Committed to this very new way of living, she immersed herself (and continues to do so) in plant-based learning.

Proudly, she continues to expand her knowledge of plant-based nutrition. Tiffany is a Licensed Food For Life Instructor with the Physicians Committee For Responsible Medicine. She is a graduate of the T. Colin Campbell Center for Nutrition Studies from eCornell University earning a Plant-Based Certification and received her Culinary Plant Based Certification from Rouxbe Forks Over Knives Culinary School. She has also obtained her Certified Health/Wellness Coach Certification with an emphasis on Lifestyle Medicine from WellCoaches School of Coaching, as well as completing her Culinary Health Education Coaching Certification from Harvard University.

When asked what is something she is most grateful for, she left us speechless. “Honestly, although I am grateful for many things in my life, I am most grateful that I have been able to transition my family and others to this lifestyle. It's been quite incredible to see amazing changes in their lives by simply changing what is placed in their grocery cart." 

As for her mission in life, it is this.... "If I can give any and everyone the tools to live lives full of vitality and health through knowledge of nutrition, my smile is complete. The goal is to change at least one life a day, and I will continue to keep that as my goal for as long as I am here to do so."

 Instagram https://www.instagram.com/inviteplants2everymeal/

Facebook -https://www.facebook.com/Invite-Plants-To-Every-Meal-101869161549681

Her website is https://inviteplantstoeverymeal.com/

She will be teaching upcoming PCRM classes starting in January 2022. The first series class is a Food for Life 21 Day Vegan Kickstart Challenge where you will be immersed into whole food plant based eating for 21 days.  Get ready for delicious cook-a-longs, nutrition information, dialogue and more over the course of the class weekly.

Meal Prep Mason Jar Meals with Tiffany Wilkerson -- Invite Plants To Every Meal

Recipe # 1   Smoky BBQ Salad in a Jar 

Ingredients:
2 cups cooked quinoa (about 1 cup dry)
Smoked BBQ Salad Dressing(see recipe)
1 cup black beans, drained and rinsed
1 medium to large tomato, diced
1 medium-sized red onion, diced
fresh spinach

First, divide and layer the Smoky BBQ Salad Dressing among the jars at the bottom. Then, layer with 1 cup of quinoa, 1 cup beans, onions and tomatoes. Your mason jar will end with spinach on top, or your choice of greens. 

Smoky BBQ Salad Dressing

Ingredients:
3 cups of garbanzo beans(drained and rinsed)
1/4 cup tahini
1/4 cup BBQ sauce- sugar and salt free-- I use Well Your World BBQ sauce
2 Tablespoons of Smoked Paprika
1.5 cups Chili Powder
2 cloves of peeled garlic 
1/4 cup or less of freshly squeezed lemon
Water, veggie broth or aquafaba to use for blending consistency

Directions:

In a food processor, blend garbanzo beans using water, broth or aquafaba to reach a hummus texture. Once reached, slowly add in tahini, smoked paprika, chili powder, garlic, lemon, bbq sauce and continue to blend. Ideally, you are looking for salad dressing consistency. 

Recipe # 2   Breakfast Burrito in a Jar

Ingredients:
2 cups salsa
1 cup black beans
1 cup of roasted sweet potatoes
1 large red bell pepper, chopped
1 cup green onions
Top with tortilla strips

Instructions:

First, fill each jar with 1 cup of salsa. Then, add 1/2 cup of black beans, roasted sweet potatoes, 1/2 cup of green onion, 1/2 cup red bell pepper.

Tortilla Strips:

Thinly slice one corn tortilla into strips. Place onto a baking sheet and spritz with veggie broth or lime juice. Lightly sprinkle with cumin or your choice of seasoning. Bake at 350 for 8 to 12 minutes, oven time may vary. Keep close watch.

Recipe # 3

Sushi in a Jar

Ingredients:
1 cup short grain brown rice
3 to 4 nori sheets, thinly sliced
1 Tablespoon of unseasoned rice vinegar
1/2 cup shredded carrots
1/2 cup cucumbers
1/2 cup red bell peppers
1/2 cup roasted sweet potatoes 

Directions:

1. Season warm cooked rice with unseasoned vinegar

2. Layer jars with rice first, then proceed to layer cucumbers, red bell peppers, carrots, sweet potatoes. Place seaweed strips in between each layer, or as desired.

snippet.title: Marital Skill Gaps for Generation X and Millennials (and Generation Y) | S2E2 - Clip 11
snippet.description:
Available as a Podcast: https://mrhomemakerpodcast.buzzsprout.com/1928223/11315630-marriage-purpose-of-s2e2-2022-09-14 
*
Mr. Homemaker's Definition & Purpose of MARRIAGE [Draft as of Sep 14, 2022]:
Exclusivity (especially Sexual Exclusivity)
Commitment (e.g. life-long)
Mutual Enrichment
Joint Endeavor to Bring up Children & Foster a Well-Functioning Family
*
References
Seven Types of Love: https://www.psychologytoday.com/us/blog/hide-and-seek/201606/these-are-the-7-types-love;
Family & Marriage: The Functionalist Perspective on the Family – ReviseSociology;
Why Bother with Marriage: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kp4FLeY6F9g;
US Supreme Court on Marriage: https://www.law.cornell.edu/supremecourt/text/14-556;  
*

Classical Philosophy distinguishes six kinds of “love” relevant to our model of Marriage - Romantic Love being just one kind.
(1) Passionate Love [Eros] entails sexual “Romantic Love”
(2) Playful, Uncommitted Love [Ludus] entails fun and/or conquest - with no strings attached
(3) Friendship [Philia] entails mutual good will
Utility
Pleasure
Mutual Admiration = Goodness = Rationality & Virtue
(4) Familial Love [Storge] is like Friendship, but rooted in dependency, familiarity, and our identity (rather than qualities)
(5) Practical Love [Pragma] is founded on reason, duty, and long-term interests
(6) Universal Love (e.g. of strangers) [Agape] is unselfish, altruistic concern for welfare of other [generally]
*
United States Supreme Court (SCOTUS) Opinion, OBERGEFELL v. HODGES - 

Four principles and traditions demonstrate that the reasons marriage is fundamental under the Constitution -
1. personal choice regarding marriage is inherent in the concept of individual autonomy;
2. a two-person union unlike any other in its importance to the committed individuals;
3. it safeguards children and families and thus draws meaning from related rights of childrearing, procreation, and education;

(a) Without the recognition, stability, and predictability marriage offers, children suffer the stigma of knowing their families are somehow lesser. 
(b) They also suffer the significant material costs of being raised by unmarried parents, relegated to a more difficult and uncertain family life.
(c) Precedent protects the right of a married couple not to procreate, so the right to marry cannot be conditioned on the capacity or commitment to procreate.
4. marriage is a keystone of the Nation’s social order…. States have plac[ed] it at the center of many facets of the legal and social order.
*
TED: Rethinking infidelity ... a talk for anyone who has ever loved at https://www.ted.com/talks/esther_perel_rethinking_infidelity_a_talk_for_anyone_who_has_ever_loved 
*

Christine Rose is an internationally bestselling author, speaker and coach, Forbes Coaches Council Member, Certified Value Builder Advisor, Certified Core Values Coach, and Certified Psychological Safety Coach featured on Forbes, Public Interest Radio, and National Business Radio. She coaches CEOs to grow leadership, effective teams, and high value companies.

A great coach have more than answers - they connect with the right questions
Christine loves to do deep dives with her clients, peels back coaching sessions like an onion to see what’s beyond ‘that’
Ask your coach what your ROI going to be, then ask them to prove and justify the ROI
Christine loves to truly and authentically connect with her clients by creating a safe space a cozy cocoon
Take 100% responsibility for the future you want to create for yourself

snippet.title: Black-Eyed Pea & Collard Green Soup and Persimmon Pudding | Chef AJ LIVE! with Carolyn Strickland
snippet.description:
5 DELICIOUS DINNER RECIPES to support your weight loss: https://www.chefaj.com/5-delicious-low-fat-dinner-recipes
-------------------------------------------------------------------------
To subscribe to their YouTube channel: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCqZuIQgZla3Qfl5575urHxQ

Carolyn May Strickland from Montgomery, Alabama, is a Food for Life Instructor with Physicians Committee for Responsible Medicine (PCRM). She's also a Licensed Independent Clinical Social Worker, certified fitness instructor, graduate of Cornell Center for Nutrition Studies, and graduate of Rouxbe/Forks Over Knives plant-based cooking school. 

Carolyn and her husband Bryan, a primary care physician, have been plant-based since 2009. They recently started a YouTube channel called Stricklands & Friends: A Seasonal WFPB Cookbook, and they will have video content available soon on UnChained TV!

You can also see Carolyn on the PlantBased Network show, So Many Cooks in the Kitchen, and you’ll find some of her original recipes featured in Glen Merzer’s new book Food is Climate.  Carolyn and her husband started a monthly plant-based potluck, VegOut, in 2013 and a monthly cooking and nutrition class, Meatless Monday Montgomery, at the Montgomery Whole Foods in 2018.  Carolyn enjoys teaching health and nutrition classes in her community at the YMCA, in churches, for local community organizations, and for private groups. 

Follow Carolyn on Instagram https://www.instagram.com/tofuchic/ and https://www.instagram.com/meatless_monday_montgomery/ and on Facebook athttps://www.facebook.com/VegOutMontgomery and https://www.facebook.com/search/top/?q=Meatless%20Monday%20Montgomery.

Black-Eyed Pea and Collard Green Soup
Ingredients: 
  2 15-ounce cans low sodium black-eyed peas, rinsed/drained (or 3 cups)
  3-4 T vegetable broth for cooking sausage
  1 large yellow onion, chopped
  2 cloves garlic, finely chopped
  ½ package (2 links) Field Roast Smoked Apple Sage “sausage” crumbled or sliced (or similar product, such as Tofurky)- optional
  2 stalks celery, chopped
  6 cups low-sodium vegetable or non-chicken broth (I like Better Than Bouillon or homemade)
  1 large bunch collard greens, tough stems and ribs removed, leaves thinly sliced or torn into bite-size pieces (about 4 cups)
  4 carrots, chopped
  ¼-½ t liquid smoke
  ¼-½ t smoked paprika
       Ground black pepper to taste
  Cayenne pepper to taste
Method: 
Heat a stock pot over medium heat. Add crumbed/sliced Field Roast sausage and a few T broth and cook until lightly browned (2 or 3 minutes). Set aside in a bowl. In the same stock pot, over medium-high heat, sauté onion, garlic, and celery until onion is translucent, 5 to 8 minutes, adding water/broth a few T at a time to keep veggies from sticking to pan.  Add drained and rinsed black eyed peas and broth and bring to a boil.  Add collard greens, carrots, liquid smoke, and smoked paprika - simmer until tender, at least 20 to 30 minutes more.  Add cooked sausage and simmer 10 minutes. Season with pepper and cayenne and serve. (This soup is even better the next day!) 

Persimmon Pudding

3 persimmons (peeling makes the pudding a little smoother)
2 pitted dates
½ cup raw cashews (boiled for 10 minutes if you don’t have a high-speed blender)
½ t ground cinnamon (or more if desired)
¼ t ground nutmeg (more if desired)
splash of lemon juice
a few tablespoons of almond milk

Whirl all ingredients in high speed blender until well blended. Pour into bowls and serve immediately, or cover and place in refrigerator to chill before serving

recipe by Carolyn Strickland

Nutrition Facts
Calories 181, Calories from Fat 40, Total Fat 4.5g, Trans Fat 0.0g, Cholesterol 0mg, Sodium 700mg, Potassium 326mg, Total Carbohydrates 26.5g, Dietary Fiber 7.5g, Sugars 3.4g, Protein 13.0g
Vitamin A 134%, Vitamin C 28%, Calcium 9%, Iron 12%
Nutrition Grade A
 • Low in saturated fat
 • No cholesterol
 • High in dietary fiber
 • High in thiamin
 • Very high in vitamin A, High in vitamin C   recipe by Carolyn Strickland

-------------------------------------------------------------------------

TIME CODE:
00:00 Guest introduction and Food for Life discussion 
0:04:02 Chef AJ Q & A with cooking demo - Black-Eyed Pea & Collard Green Soup and Persimmon Pudding 
0:35:56 Carolyn's husband Bryan joins the show
0:45:00 What Carolyn and her husband eat on a daily basis
0:48:04 More Chef AJ and viewer Q & A
1:20:22 Final thoughts and show wrap

snippet.title: Want to attend an all expenses paid 3-week introductory summer scholarship flight training program?
snippet.description:
Joining me to talk about the program in detail are Captain Justin, the AIM HIGH Flight Academy Program Director, Air Force Recruiting Services Detachment 1; Lieutenant Colonel Kelly McNerney, Director of Operations, Air Force Recruiting Services Detachment 1; 1st Lieutenant Courtney Tomas, a KC-135R/T Stratotanker pilot currently stationed at Fairchild Air Force Base, Washington; and Victoria Johnson, a Certified Flight Instructor at the University of North Dakota Aerospace Foundation.

The Aim High Flight Academy (AHFA) is an aviation scholarship for a three-week introductory flight program at various colleges and universities across the country.   

The primary objectives of the flight school are to ensure selected students understand the pathway to becoming an Air Force rated officer, and to aid the increase of an individual’s Pilot Candidate Selection Method score with the completion of up to 15 flight hours.

AFHA pairs unaffiliated youth and cadets nominated by USAFA and AFROTC with Total Force Officers who provide aviation career broadening and mentorship about the various aviation professions and career fields that can be potential pathways to achieving their goals.

All Things Aviation & Aerospace is an aviation career oriented webcast/podcast providing insight on the variety of opportunities and possibilities in the aviation and aerospace industries. It's host and moderator Vince Mickens, is a long time private pilot who flies frequently for both personal and business. Mickens’ background includes 17 years in aviation advocacy executive roles with the National Business Aviation Association (NBAA), the Aircraft Owners and Pilots Association (AOPA), and the Bob Hoover Legacy Foundation, all after a 28-year broadcast journalism career working in seven major television and radio broadcast markets nationwide.
